evidence.description: Sistani adheres to the quietist Najaf tradition and rejects the doctrine of velayat-e faqih (Guardianship of the Jurist) championed by Khomeini and institutionalized in the Islamic Republic of Iran. This theological and political opposition is a defining feature of Sistani's approach.
Sistani's quietist approach stands in fundamental contrast to the activist political role for clerics advocated by Khomeini.

editorial_notes: Sistani's opposition is doctrinal — his quietism vs. Khomeini's velayat-e faqih — rather than a direct personal confrontation. Both figures are deceased or active in different eras.
